# `@oai/sky`

Model-facing computer use API

See [docs](./docs/) for generated documentation based on library source.

- [Linux](./docs/sky-full-desktop-api.md)
- [macOS](./docs/sky-window-api.md)
- [Windows](./docs/sky-window2-api.md)

## usage

```js
import { sky } from "@oai/sky";

await sky.click({ x: 200, y: 300 });

await sky.type_text({ text: "banana" });
```

### configuration

Internally `sky` client will create the correct client based on the detected `process.platform`.
If present, `sky` will read the JSON config file at `OAI_SKY_CONFIG_PATH` for host runtime configuration.
Model-authored code should not choose a target or pass target options.

```bash
export OAI_SKY_CONFIG_PATH=/path/to/sky.json
```

```json
{
  "target": "linux",
  // Milliseconds to wait after successful input actions so the desktop can process and repaint. Set 0 to disable. (default: 100)
  "post_action_sleep_ms": 100
}
```

`sky` reads the config lazily.
Importing `@oai/sky` does not initialize the client, the first actual use of `sky` does.
The configured `target` controls the available API.

#### window mode

Configure `target: "mac"` or `target: "windows"` to use the window API.
Window actions return `{ app, screenshot: { url } | null, text }`.

```js
const apps = await sky.list_apps();

const state = await sky.get_app_state({ app: "Weather" });

await sky.click({
  app: "Weather",
  element_index: 1,
});

await sky.click({
  app: "Weather",
  x: 200,
  y: 300,
  mouse_button: "left",
  click_count: 2,
});

await sky.drag({
  app: "Weather",
  from_x: 200,
  from_y: 300,
  to_x: 400,
  to_y: 500,
});

await sky.perform_secondary_action({
  app: "Weather",
  element_index: 1,
  action: "showMenu",
});

await sky.press_key({
  app: "Weather",
  key: "Return",
});

await sky.scroll({
  app: "Weather",
  element_index: 1,
  direction: "down",
  pages: 0.5,
});

await sky.set_value({
  app: "Weather",
  element_index: 2,
  value: "Boston",
});

await sky.type_text({
  app: "Weather",
  text: "Boston",
});
```

#### full desktop mode

Configure `target: "linux"` to use the full desktop API.

```js
const screenshots = await sky.get_screenshot();

await sky.click({
  x: 200,
  y: 300,
});

await sky.click({
  x: 200,
  y: 300,
  mouse_button: "right",
  click_count: 1,
});

await sky.drag({
  from_x: 200,
  from_y: 300,
  to_x: 400,
  to_y: 500,
});

await sky.press_key({ key: "Return" });

await sky.scroll({
  direction: "down",
  pages: 0.5,
});

await sky.scroll({
  direction: "up",
  x: 200,
  y: 300,
});

await sky.type_text({ text: "banana" });
```

## build

`pnpm tarball` does the host-side prep first, then calls `js build project=@oai/sky`.
The prep step syncs the cached Windows binary from Azure, verifies the binaries
that should be included, and optionally rebuilds the platform-specific binaries.

```bash
# default
# - az_sync for cached windows binary
# - verify bin/linux
# - verify bin/windows
# - build the tarball
pnpm tarball
```

### ci

Make changes and [increment the version in `package.json`](https://semver.org/)

CI will publish the library to both [artifactory](https://applied-jfrog.gateway.ci-0.internal.api.openai.org/ui/repos/tree/General/npm-local/@oai/sky/-/@oai) and [azure blobstore](http://go/azv/az://appliedciblobdata/data/oai-js/registry/@oai/sky).

### windows

> http://go/docs-link/oai_sky_js_bin

Windows binary is expected at `bin/windows/codex-computer-use.exe`.
Use `OAI_SKY_TARBALL_WINDOWS` to control how this behaves during `pnpm tarball`.

```bash
# sync, verify, and include the cached windows binary
OAI_SKY_TARBALL_WINDOWS="cache" pnpm tarball

# rebuild the windows binary locally, then include it (requires running on Windows)
OAI_SKY_TARBALL_WINDOWS="build" pnpm tarball

# exclude the windows binary
OAI_SKY_TARBALL_WINDOWS="skip" pnpm tarball
```

#### updating `bin/windows`

Refresh `bin/windows/codex-computer-use.exe` from the executable artifact produced
by the GitHub Action below.

https://github.com/openai/openai/actions/workflows/applied-tf-windows-computer-use-plugin-publish.yaml

Download the `oai-sky-0.4.1-<timestamp>-<sha>.tgz.zip` artifact, then provide
the local path to the artifact and exact git commit sha. You may also pass an
already unzipped `oai-sky-0.4.1-<timestamp>-<sha>.tgz`.

```bash
python -m sky_js.upload_bins \
tarball_path=/Users/noah/Downloads/oai-sky-0.4.1-26196911761-30acaae3cfad.tgz.zip \
git_sha=30acaae3cfadc2464d94885121d410b17c7da805
```

```
[upload_bins] unzip oai-sky-0.4.1-26196911761-30acaae3cfad.tgz.zip
[upload_bins] untar oai-sky-0.4.1-26196911761-30acaae3cfad.tgz
[upload_bins] bin/windows/codex-computer-use.exe → az://appliedciblobdata/data/cua/sky_js/upload_bins/30acaae3cfadc2464d94885121d410b17c7da805/bin/windows/codex-computer-use.exe
```

Update the `WINDOWS_AZ_SYNC` git sha in [`az_sync.py`](./sky_js/az_sync.py).

```
az://appliedciblobdata/data/cua/sky_js/upload_bins/30acaae3cfadc2464d94885121d410b17c7da805/bin/windows/codex-computer-use.exe
```

### linux

> http://go/docs-link/oai_sky_js_bin

Linux binaries are expected at `bin/linux/sky_linux_arm64` and `bin/linux/sky_linux_x64`.
Use `OAI_SKY_TARBALL_LINUX` to control how this behaves during `pnpm tarball`.

```bash
# verify and include existing linux binaries
OAI_SKY_TARBALL_LINUX="cache" pnpm tarball

# rebuild linux binaries locally, then include them (requires docker)
OAI_SKY_TARBALL_LINUX="build" pnpm tarball

# exclude linux binaries
OAI_SKY_TARBALL_LINUX="skip" pnpm tarball
```

#### updating `bin/linux`

No-op. `bin/linux` is fully automated: Buildkite builds the `arm64` and `x64`
executables from `sky_linux`, downloads those artifacts into this package, and
then builds the `@oai/sky` tarball. Use `OAI_SKY_TARBALL_LINUX="build"` only when
you need to reproduce that flow locally.

### test

```bash
pnpm test:build
```

## unified container test

Build `sky_linux` for `arm64` locally

```bash
python -m sky_linux.build output_dir="project/cua/sky_js/bin/linux" target=arm64

cd ~/project/cua/sky_js
pnpm tarball
```

Add the tarball generated by above under `project/cua/sky_js` to the bottom of `caas-desktop` stage of [`project/cua/containers/chrome/Dockerfile`](../containers/chrome/Dockerfile)

```dockerfile
FROM caas-next-agent AS caas-desktop
# ...
COPY "project/cua/sky_js/sky_js-1.0.0-202605112135-noahj.tgz" /opt/localnpms/
```

Start unified container locally to test end-to-end

```bash
cd ~/project/cua/containers/chrome
TARGET="caas-desktop" make dev
```

Once the container is started, open a separate terminal and run commands below

```bash
cd ~/project/cua/containers/chrome
make shell
# run inside container
# start container services
# run novnc with visible cursor (makes localization easier for human)
CUA_DD_X11VNC_ARGS="-cursor arrow -nocursorshape -nocursorpos" dev
```

Back to the initial `make dev` shell and run `node` in `/home/oai` to open a node repl and test `@oai/sky`

```bash
cd /home/oai
node
```

<!-- prettier-ignore -->
```javascript
m = await import("@oai/sky")

[screenshot] = await m.sky.get_screenshot()

// open the logged url in browser on your host machine to view image
console.log(`http://localhost:8080/file?path=${screenshot.filepath}`)

// click Home folder
await m.sky.click({ x: 50, y: 50 })
// click desktop
await m.sky.click({ x: 200, y: 200 })

// drag Home folder to middle of desktop
await m.sky.drag({ from_x: 50, from_y: 50, to_x: 400, to_y: 400 })
// drag it back
await m.sky.drag({ from_x: 400, from_y: 400, to_x: 50, to_y: 50 })
```

## faq

### `package.json` `main`

The checked-in `package.json` points at source files:

```json
{
  "main": "src/index.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": "./src/index.ts"
  }
}
```

This is source metadata for local development and for the `oai_js` build input. The default
`oai_js` build temporarily rewrites the packed `package.json` to point at emitted JavaScript under
`dist/project/cua/sky_js/src/`, then restores the checked-in `package.json` after packing. Consumers
of the built package load the `dist/**/*.js` entrypoints from the tarball, not the checked-in
`src/**/*.ts` paths.

When debugging package-resolution issues, inspect the tarball instead of only reading the checked-in
`package.json`:

```bash
tar -xOf sky_js-*.tgz package/package.json
tar -xOf sky_js-*.tgz package/dist/project/cua/sky_js/src/sky.js
```
